core_scenarios.description: Greenfield project full UI taste exploration. User describes a weather app idea. Step 0c detects frontend involvement, asks platform preference (iOS). System identifies 3 key pages (home, detail, settings). For each page: generates 5 variants via Stitch MCP (Gemini 3.1 Pro), downloads HTML, post-processes (font patch to PingFang SC, asset inlining), starts local preview server. User switches between iPhone/iPad device sizes, uses side-by-side comparison, selects variant for home page. System extracts Design DNA, creates design system, subsequent pages auto-inherit style. User provides text feedback on home page variant ('make the header more compact'), system regenerates with constraint applied. User selects 2 variants for hybrid DNA analysis, annotates preferred dimensions, system synthesizes composite constraint. Iteration history preserved — user can rollback to any previous round. After finalizing home page, system auto-generates dark/light counterpart; user toggles between modes in preview, accepts or requests re-generation. All pages selected: Design tokens exported to specs/mockups/tokens.json (DTCG format), selected mockups saved to specs/mockups/, constraints written to discover.json, temp files cleaned up. Discover flow continues to Layer 1.

Existing project new page addition. User runs /discover in an existing React project with tailwind.config.js and established component library. Step 0c detects existing frontend. System reads tailwind.config.js and existing CSS, extracts style profile (color palette, spacing, border-radius, font stack). Synthesizes designMd constraint. Generates 5 variants in REFINE mode (subtle variations within established style). User selects variant consistent with existing app. New page mockup saved alongside design tokens compatible with existing system.

Stitch unavailable degradation. User has not configured Stitch API key. System detects Stitch MCP unavailable, switches to Tier 2 (AgentHTMLProvider) within 5 seconds. Agent generates 3-5 HTML mockups using designMd constraints for quality (correct layout, coordinated colors, basic animation). Same preview engine serves the variants. User selects. Tokens exported as CSS custom properties (simplified format). Mid-session quota variant: Stitch available initially, quota exhausted on page 3 of 5. System notifies user, switches to Tier 2 for remaining pages.
